figures.caption: Table 1: Main results on seven open-domain QA benchmarks (Exact Match). All retrieval-augmented baselines use 3B-parameter LLMs for fair comparison. PL-Search-Base/Instruct apply our self-bootstrapping pipeline (SFT on filtered seed trajectories followed by plan-aware RL refinement) to Qwen2.5-3B-Base/Instruct; the SFT only row reports the same target after SFT but before RL refinement. Bold indicates the best result in each column. Avg. columns report per-category and overall averages.
Table 9: A representative trajectory generated by our method, illustrating plan-based multi-hop reasoning. Key evidence is highlighted in blue.

Table 4: Self-bootstrapping compared with strong-model distillation as the SFT data source, evaluated across three target models. Both variants use the same SFT-then-RL pipeline and 4,500 correctness-filtered trajectories, differing only in the data generator (Self-Bootstrapping Qwen2.5-3B-base vs. Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct). The evaluation metric is Exact Match; bold indicates the better result within each model group. † means RL training with 72B-distilled SFT initialization collapsed in multiple runs for Qwen2.5-7B-Base and Qwen3-4B-Base; reported numbers are best checkpoints before collapse.

Table 3: Performance comparison (EM) across model families and scales. “AutoRefine” refers to the searchand-refine-during-think baseline, and “Ours” refers to our plan method. Bold indicates the higher value between AutoRefine and our method.

Table 12: Detailed performance comparison (EM, F1, CEM) across model families and scales. “AutoRefine” refers to the search-and-refine-during-think baseline, and “Ours” refers to our plan-and-refine method. For Qwen2.5-3BInstruct, F1 and CEM are not reported for AutoRefine (shown as “—”) because the original paper (Shi et al., 2025) only reported EM for this configuration.

Figure 4: Failure modes of direct RL on the plan task across four configurations (R: Refine, P: Plan). (a) Training entropy: only Qwen2.5-3B-Base under the R → P order trains stably, whereas the other three runs exhibit prior collapse, entropy explosion, or volatile training under the reversed order. (b) These entropy dynamics correspond directly to downstream performance: only the stable run yields sustained EM gains, while the others either fail to improve or collapse at the step where their entropy breaks down.

Figure 2: Overview of our framework. Given a user question, the policy model first generates a global plan decomposing the question into sub-questions, then iteratively executes think, search, and refine for each subquestion before producing the final answer. Training is driven by a composite reward combining plan, format, and outcome rewards, with the loss masked over retrieved documents.
Table 11: Representative failure trajectories under direct RL training on the plan task.
Table 5: Hyperparameters for RL training and SFT.
